justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVE flagged as Critical, all 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string, left-pad, moment), safe_to_deploy is false, and severity ratings are well-differentiated (Critical, High, Important, Suggestion levels).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with clear reproduction steps showing how breaking changes auto-install
jsonwebtoken CVE-2022-23529 specifically named with correct version threshold (<9.0.0)
crypto-random-string ESM incompatibility correctly identified with the specific error (ERR_REQUIRE_ESM) and practical fix (downgrade to v4 or use native crypto)
left-pad supply chain risk identified with historical context (2016 incident) and native alternative (padStart)
moment.js deprecation correctly flagged at lower severity than the critical issues
Bonus finding: missing lockfile issue correctly identified as compounding the wildcard version risk
Actionable fixes provided for every finding with realistic time estimates
crypto-random-string labeled 'High' rather than mapping to the project's severity vocabulary — minor inconsistency but the intent is correct
